ogTitle: Waratah West vs Wardell: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Waratah West and Wardell.
heading: Waratah West vs Wardell — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Waratah West, New South Wales and Wardell, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Wardell has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to Waratah West (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Waratah West or Wardell safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Waratah West has a flood risk score of 43/100, while Wardell scores 21/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Waratah West scores 62/100 for bushfire risk. Wardell scores 36/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Waratah West or Wardell?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Wardell has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.12% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
